signals.title: USD/JPY — Fed Hawkishness + Carry + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.74 testing levels above the 160.00 intervention threshold as Fed Chairman Warsh signals a tighter policy path. Despite BoJ verbal warnings, the +275bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.98%) sustains the bid. Retail sentiment is 85% short, diverging from the institutional carry-driven trend; this extreme retail shorting increases the risk of a squeeze higher toward 163.00 unless the BoJ intervenes. JPY COT LF net short -115,036 reflects crowded carry positioning.

signals.title: GBP/CHF — G10 Bilateral Theme + Political Shock
signals.text: GBP/CHF at 1.0704 reflects a dominant G10 bilateral theme as GBP outperforms despite PM Starmer's resignation. The pair is supported by a massive +375bp carry premium (BoE 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%) and a restrictive real rate of +0.77% in the UK vs -0.60% in Switzerland. Options market remains cautious with a -0.82 moderate put skew on GBP/USD, suggesting hedging against political volatility even as the carry trade remains intact.

signals.title: USD/CAD — COT vs Retail Divergence + CPI Risk
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4155 shows extreme retail vs institutional divergence ahead of Canada's May CPI release. Retail traders are 95% short (extreme), fading the move, while COT LF net positioning is -60,671 (specs short CAD). The +150bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%) favors USD. A higher-than-expected CPI print is required to trigger a retail-led reversal; otherwise, the path of least resistance remains higher toward 1.4200.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + Positioning Stall
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1464 exhibits an elevated vol risk premium (VRP) of +2.3%, with IV at 7.1% vs HV30 at 4.8%. This suggests the options market is pricing in significant protection relative to recent realized movement. COT LF net short -49,290 aligns with the +150bp USD carry advantage. The negative 25d RR (-0.67) confirms a persistent put skew, indicating that despite the 1W flat performance, the market remains biased toward EUR downside protection.

signals.title: USD/CAD vs WTI — Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/CAD vs WTI Oil correlation has decoupled to -0.512 vs a 252d norm of -0.005 (z = -1.95σ). The Canadian Dollar is failing to respond to oil price fluctuations, with the pair instead being driven by the +150bp Fed-BoC carry spread and hawkish Fed rhetoric. This break suggests CAD has lost its commodity-link sensitivity in the current high-yield USD regime. Watch for a return to norm if US yields stabilize.
